settings.question: Who is eligible to buy from Brant Business Interiors under OECM Agreement 2025-470?
settings.answer: Any OECM-eligible Ontario organization: school boards (K-12), publicly-assisted colleges and universities, hospitals and CCACs, municipalities and their agencies, broader-public-sector organizations, and not-for-profits in social services and education. OECM publishes the full eligible-buyer list on their site. Not sure if your organization qualifies? Call 1-800-835-9565 and we'll confirm in five minutes.

settings.question: Do I still need to run an RFP under Agreement 2025-470?
settings.answer: No. OECM has already run the procurement on your behalf — pricing, warranty terms, delivery windows, and product compliance are all pre-negotiated at the master-agreement level. Your organization's purchasing policy still applies (signing limits, board approvals, internal PO authorization), but the 6–8 weeks normally spent issuing an RFP, evaluating bids, and writing a contract is what disappears.

settings.question: Which categories and brands are covered under Agreement 2025-470?
settings.answer: The full Brant Business Interiors furniture catalog — seating, desks and workstations, tables, storage and filing, panels and dividers, and accessories. See the coverage table on this page for storefront-callable brand × category counts. 25+ additional Agreement-eligible manufacturer lines (Lacasse, Safco, ergoCentric, Keilhauer, and others) are quoted on request.

settings.question: How fast is the quote turnaround?
settings.answer: One business day on most line-item quotes. Larger projects with a free design-layout component typically take 5 business days. You'll be talking to the same Ontario account team that owns your file through install and warranty — not an offshore quote desk.

settings.question: Is the OECM price always the lowest available?
settings.answer: Honestly, not always — an open-tender bid on a single very-high-volume SKU can occasionally beat the OECM rate. But once you factor in tender cost, evaluation time, and the risk of a non-compliant low bidder, OECM nets out cheaper on the vast majority of orders. For very large fit-outs we'll tell you directly if running a separate tender would save you money.

settings.question: How do delivery and installation work?
settings.answer: Our own crew handles delivery, assembly, levelling, and removal of old furniture — we don't subcontract logistics. After-hours and weekend installs are available for occupied spaces, which matters for schools and hospitals. W5-cleared installers for ministry and hospital sites.

settings.question: What's the warranty handling under OECM?
settings.answer: Same as any Brant Business Interiors order — we file the manufacturer claim on your behalf and keep the piece in service. Multi-year warranties across Global, OTG, Heartwood, ergoCentric, and the rest of our authorized lines; specific terms vary by manufacturer. Loaner chairs available during repair. One service contact, Ontario-based.

settings.question: Can I add non-OECM-eligible items to the same PO?
settings.answer: Yes. We can split a single quote into the Agreement 2025-470-eligible portion (drawn against the master agreement) and any out-of-scope items handled under your normal procurement. You get one delivery, one install, one invoice — procurement tracking lists the two streams.

settings.hero_heading: Buy commercial office furniture under OECM Agreement 2025-470 — no separate RFP.
settings.hero_standfirst: Brant Business Interiors is registered with OECM under our parent entity, Brant Basics, as an authorized Supplier Partner under Agreement 2025-470. Ontario's broader public sector — school boards, hospitals, municipalities, colleges, universities, and not-for-profits — can buy our full furniture catalog at OECM-negotiated terms without running a separate tender. One quote, one PO, one invoice.
